title: Topic Undefined
wikitext:
[]: Topic: Undefined             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Oct 04, 2005 12:24 pm Here goes :P A question that has always dogged me during the aftermath of The End of Evangelion was why Shinji and Asuka were together. I think I have the answer. (well its a matter of perception, or level of intoxication in some cases :o) Gendo says that Instrumentality is a â€œreturn to a state of beginning.â€ This implies a return to the womb. We see souls re-entering the (artifical?) Chamber of Gauf within Lilith. So instrumentality is a return to the womb of the ultimate mother. Later we see Shinji and Asuka together on the beach. What similarity do they both share? Both of their mothers souls are â€˜trappedâ€™ within the Evangelions. Shinji and Asukaâ€™s soul are not able to regress back to Lilith due to a â€˜missing linkâ€™ I believe the Angels were born from Adam, and in order for them to succeed in reaching the same state they seek Adam out. Maybe each Angel gave birth to the next? Imagine it like a collapsible telescope. Another good way to imagine it would be the branches of a tree. (this will sound odd but bear with me) If the tree wanted to return to a seed-like state the leaves would regress into the branch, the branches would regress into the trunk and so on. I believe that the Tree of Life represents just that. And besides, how are the particles in the leaves gonna get back without the branches to guide them? So what Iâ€™m saying here is that the â€˜souls guidance systemâ€™ or the â€˜souls road back to Lilithâ€™ has been fucked up by both their mothers souls being inaccessible. Hopefully this image will help explain: (it took ages to make) This sounds clear to me, however I replaced most of my brain-synapses with drugs so any questions to help make my point of view more comprehensible are cool. Of course a good counter-agruement is that Shinji made this decision. But did he? Unit-01 is God, Shinji is just the pilot in the end. Now that I have flown high its time for you to shoot me down ; )

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Oct 04, 2005 1:05 pm Why are their souls inaccessable?? Why is this different from any other 'mothers' who have died in the past?? I don't get this--it is pretty clear why they are there--they both have the will to return--Shinji rejects instrumentality explicitly and Asuka's rebirth fuels her with the desire to live on--"I don't wanna die!!!!!!!" as she says herself...

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Oct 04, 2005 1:23 pm Shinji & Asuka both have their mother's soul inside an Evangelion. Nobody else we see has this in common with one another. They are the only ones left afterwards... The connection is like... clear dude. The whole ripping the soul from the body must somehow 'block' the path. At least this is what I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Oct 04, 2005 1:32 pm Zugzwang wrote: Shinji & Asuka both have their mother's soul inside an Evangelion. Nobody else we see has this in common with one another. They are the only ones left afterwards... The connection is like... clear dude. The whole ripping the soul from the body must somehow 'block' the path. At least this is what I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by geishaboy » Tue Oct 04, 2005 10:41 pm Thats quite a good angle actually. Like most of evangelion, it's open to interpretation. I mean, there are quite a few angles you can take. One of my favourites is that Shinji and Asuka can back for each other , but that might just be fan service more than anything. For me, I don't really find the specifics of why it was only those two that remain to be overly important. It just feels right, so, yeah, I just go with that. I think a lot of the mysteries in Evangelion can only be understood with feelings and emotions, watching Evangelion for me was quite similar to feeling my way down a dark hallway, untill I finally found the door at the other end.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:03 am Thing is, we don't even know that they were the only ones to come back at the end. Chances are half of the human race were back where they started, while the rest were, um, back where they started, if you follow me.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:08 am If you're suggesting that Asuka and Shinji are the only ones unable to enter complementation, or that somehow it was their loss of connection to their mothers that stopped third impact, then you're probably on the wrong track. Given Rei and Yui's words in EoE: Rei:Anyone can return to human form as long as they can imagine themselves in their own heart. Yui (voice): Don't worry. All living things have the ability to return to their original form... and the heart to go on living.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:39 am "Anyone can return if they have the will..." Shinji rejected Instrumentality and therfor was the first to return. Asuka also suposevly rejected it and therfor was the second. If one rejects instrumentality when given the choice, they can return, otherwise they cannot.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Wed Oct 05, 2005 12:10 pm Tsu-chan wrote: If one rejects instrumentality when given the choice[...]

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Wed Oct 05, 2005 12:24 pm Phoenix Feet wrote: Tsu-chan wrote: If one rejects instrumentality when given the choice[...]

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 05, 2005 12:49 pm Tsu-chan wrote: "Anyone can return if they have the will..." Shinji rejected Instrumentality and therfor was the first to return. Asuka also suposevly rejected it and therfor was the second. If one rejects instrumentality when given the choice, they can return, otherwise they cannot.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Oct 05, 2005 1:17 pm A lot of evidence against it i see *laughs* I forgot about Touji. that bastard kinda ruins it huh? Well i tried. but it was a neat theory all the same I wonder how the angel's soul differ to that of humans? maybe this has something to do with where O.M.F was taking this discussion. Or are the souls in NGE a one size fits all kinda dig?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 05, 2005 1:48 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: However, you're idea about souls being "trapped" within the Evas and unable to enter complementation does have merit. There's evidence to suggest that Evangelions will not necessarily tangify simply by exposure to third impact.

[]:             Quote Post by wrack » Wed Oct 05, 2005 3:13 pm thewayneiac wrote: 3. If you are correct then Toji should be there as well, (mom in an EVA), and he isn't there.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Oct 06, 2005 4:57 pm Well it could also be explained by stating that his mother's soul was released when Unit-03 was destroyed. If one were so inclined.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Oct 06, 2005 5:28 pm I thought the clear implication in the show was the Evas require a soul, and a mothers' at that (except in the case of Rei), in order to function. Toji was chosen because his mother's soul was available and inside the Eva--but I could be wrong...

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Oct 06, 2005 6:24 pm wrack wrote: thewayneiac wrote: 3. If you are correct then Toji should be there as well, (mom in an EVA), and he isn't there.

[]:             Quote Post by Isanov » Sun Dec 18, 2005 1:40 am ... when Misato tells Shinji that all of his classmates are pilot candidates. Nerv has all of the students' mothers, souls on ice somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 18, 2005 4:05 am Isanov wrote: I suspect that the soul does not have to be a mother (there is really no evidence to this) but simply a soul that will accept the host (pilot).

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Sun Dec 18, 2005 8:31 am Isanov wrote: Unit 00 is unstable because the person who was put inside was not suicidal (as Yui and 02's mother were) but a worker who truely did have an acident. That is why he rejected Rei initially.
